de.uka.ilkd.key.strategy

Class QueueRuleApplicationManager

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.strategy.QueueRuleApplicationManager
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    NewRuleListener, AutomatedRuleApplicationManager

    public class QueueRuleApplicationManager
extends java.lang.Object
implements AutomatedRuleApplicationManager
    Implementation of AutomatedRuleApplicationManager that stores possible RuleApps in a priority queue. The element with highest priority in the queue can be obtained via next(). This operation will remove the element from the queue. The priority of a given RuleApp corresponds to its RuleAppCost. A RuleApp can be equipped with a RuleAppCost by converting it into a RuleAppContainer. The cost of a RuleApp is computed according to a given Strategy (see Feature.computeCost(RuleApp, PosInOccurrence, Goal)).

        public void rulesAdded(ImmutableList<? extends RuleApp> rules,

      • peekNext

        public RuleApp peekNext()
        Specified by:
        peekNext in interface AutomatedRuleApplicationManager
        Returns:
        the first applicable rule app, i.e. the least expensive element of the heap that is not obsolete and caches the result of this operation to save some time the next time the method nextAndCache() or next() is called. A call of next() empties the cache again.

      • next

        public RuleApp next()
        Specified by:
        next in interface AutomatedRuleApplicationManager
        Returns:
        the first applicable rule app, i.e. the least expensive element of the heap that is not obsolete
